title: FMN
description: Flavin mononucleotide (FMN), also known as riboflavin-5′-phosphate, is the bioactive form of Vitamin B2 that serves as a vital prosthetic group for mitochondrial oxidoreductases like NADH dehydrogenase. It facilitates essential electron transfer in the respiratory chain and acts as a cofactor for blue-light photoreceptors, playing a critical role in cellular energy metabolism and mitochondrial function.

research.title: Riboflavin metabolism: role in mitochondrial function
research.summary: This review details how riboflavin derivatives like FMN are indispensable for mitochondrial energy production and the maintenance of the electron transport chain.

research.title: Flavin mononucleotide
research.summary: An analysis of FMN as a stronger oxidizing agent than NAD, highlighting its unique ability to participate in both one- and two-electron transfers within cells.

sentiment.summary: Users generally view FMN as a superior, more bioavailable form of Vitamin B2 compared to standard riboflavin, specifically noting its efficacy in supporting mitochondrial health and energy levels.

effects.label: Mitochondrial Rescue:
effects.description: Highly effective in clinical contexts for treating mitochondrial diseases and ischemia/reperfusion injury.

effects.label: Bioavailability:
effects.description: Considered more effective for those with riboflavin kinase deficiencies who cannot convert standard B2 efficiently.

effects.label: Fast-Acting:
effects.description: Because it does not require the same metabolic conversion steps as riboflavin, it is utilized more rapidly by cells.

dosage.label: Common Supplement Dose:
dosage.description: Often found in 'Coenzymated' B-complexes ranging from 10mg to 50mg.

dosage.label: Clinical Application:
dosage.description: Higher therapeutic doses are used in medical settings for corneal cross-linking or specific metabolic disorders.

dosage.label: Enhanced RDA:
dosage.description: Intake often exceeds the standard 1.1-1.3mg RDA when used for targeted mitochondrial support.

sideEffects.label: Chromaturia:
sideEffects.description: Harmless bright yellow or neon-orange discoloration of urine due to excess riboflavin excretion.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Comfort:
sideEffects.description: Generally well-tolerated, though high doses may cause mild gastrointestinal upset in sensitive individuals.

sideEffects.label: Rapid Absorption:
sideEffects.description: Unlike standard B2, its high solubility can lead to faster but shorter-lived peaks in serum levels if not taken with food.

availability.label: Over-the-Counter:
availability.description: Widely available as a dietary supplement often labeled as Riboflavin-5'-Phosphate.

availability.label: FDA Approved:
availability.description: Recently approved in specific formulations (Epioxa) for medical use in treating keratoconus as of October 2025.

availability.label: Food Additive:
availability.description: Commonly found in European food products under the designation E101a.
